---
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .g-col-lg-3}
![](cdp_2025.png){.img-fluid .mb-3 .border fig-alt="Portrait of Caspar David Peter"}

::: {.text-center .mb-2}
### Caspar David Peter
::: {.subtitle .mb-2}
Associate Professor at the Accounting Department
:::
:::

::: {.profile-icons .text-center}
[{{< fa home >}}](https://www.rsm.nl/people/caspar-david-peter/ "RSM Profile")
[{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/CasparDP "GitHub")
[{{< fa envelope >}}](mailto:peter@rsm.nl "Email")
[{{< fa brands orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-0020-1673 "ORCID")
[{{< fa file-lines >}}](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/cf_dev/AbsByAuth.cfm?per_id=1589558 "SSRN")
[{{< fa file-pdf >}}](CV_academic.pdf "CV")
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8 .g-col-lg-9 .d-flex .flex-column .justify-content-start}

I study the **economic consequences of reporting transparency**: how disclosure practices shape individual and market behavior, and how they affect corporate outcomes. My research has been published in the *Journal of Financial Economics*, *The Accounting Review*, and *The Review of Financial Studies*, investigating how information environments impact corporate misconduct, industry coordination, and mergers & acquisitions.

:::

:::

## Open Projects

::: {.section-label}
Teaching materials and tools, publicly available on GitHub
:::

::: {.grid .mt-3}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.project-card}
[PhD Lecture]{.project-tag}
[Quarto]{.project-tag}

::: {.project-title}
Earnings Management Then and Now: Revisiting Jones (1991)
:::

::: {.project-desc}
An interactive PhD lecture that walks through the seminal Jones (1991) model, its evolution, and modern applications. Designed for doctoral students in accounting and finance.
:::

[Visit Site {{< fa arrow-right >}}](https://caspardp.github.io/phd-lecture-dec/){.project-link}
[Source {{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/CasparDP/phd-lecture-dec){.project-link}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.project-card}
[MSc Course]{.project-tag}
[R]{.project-tag}

::: {.project-title}
Data Analytics for Finance
:::

::: {.project-desc}
Public-facing materials for the MSc Data Analytics for Finance course at RSM. Covers data wrangling, visualization, and applied econometrics using Stata.
:::

[Visit Site {{< fa arrow-right >}}](https://caspardp.github.io/rsm-data-analytics-for-finance-public/){.project-link}
[Source {{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/CasparDP/rsm-data-analytics-for-finance-public){.project-link}
:::
:::

:::

::: {.section-label .mt-2}
[See all side projects {{< fa arrow-right >}}](sides.qmd)
:::

## Research Highlights

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .research-highlight}
::: {.card-body}
#### Owner exposure through firm disclosure

We show that companies named after their owners tend to be more opaque in their financial disclosures, particularly when revealing such information might expose sensitive owner details and attract social stigma. We also show that when disclosure is forced, these owners are more likely to change their firms' names, and new firms are less frequently named after their founding owners.

[Read Paper →](https://publications.aaahq.org/accounting-review/article-abstract/98/6/381/11037/Owner-Exposure-through-Firm-Disclosure?redirectedFrom=fulltext)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-lg-6}
::: {.card .h-100 .research-highlight}
::: {.card-body}
#### Mandatory Financial Disclosure and M&A Activity

We investigate the impact of mandatory financial disclosure on mergers and acquisitions. We find that required disclosure boosts M&A activity by reducing information frictions, as evidenced by an increase in both the number and volume of private firms becoming targets.

[Read Paper →](https://academic.oup.com/rfs/article-abstract/36/12/4788/7202058?login=false)
:::
:::
:::

:::


::: {.grid .mt-4}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
## News & Updates

::: {.news-item}
::: {.news-date}
November 2025
:::
::: {.news-title}
**New PhD lecture** on "Earnings Management Then and Now: Revisiting Jones (1991)" available. Feedback welcome. [Check it out →](https://caspardp.github.io/phd-lecture-dec/)
:::
:::

::: {.news-item}
::: {.news-date}
February 2025
:::
::: {.news-title}
**Interactive teaching materials** on financial statement analysis released. [Check it out →](https://caspardp.github.io/interactive_learning_repo/)
:::
:::

::: {.news-item}
::: {.news-date}
February 2025
:::
::: {.news-title}
**Seminar at LMU Munich** presenting research on union office closures and corporate misconduct.
:::
:::

::: {.news-item}
::: {.news-date}
January 2025
:::
::: {.news-title}
Honored to be part of the **Economenparade**, among the best published economists in the Netherlands. [Read more →](https://esb.nu/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/552-555_Parade-2024-2.pdf)
:::
:::

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
## Current Teaching

::: {.card .teaching-card .mb-3}
::: {.card-body}
#### Data & Coding
MSc Program, 2025
Coordinator and content creator

[Course Details →](teaching.qmd)
:::
:::

::: {.card .teaching-card .mb-3}
::: {.card-body}
#### Financial Reporting & Analysis
BSc Program, 2021-2025
Coordinator and instructor

[Course Details →](teaching.qmd)
:::
:::

::: {.card .teaching-card .mb-3}
::: {.card-body}
#### Accounting Analysis & Governance
MSc Program, 2025
Coordinator and instructor

[Course Details →](teaching.qmd)
:::
:::

:::

:::
